info.title: NAP Control Plane API
info.description:
REST API for the NAP control plane.

## Authentication

Requests are authenticated with a NAP Service Token. Create one at [NAP > Integration > Service Tokens](/integration/tokens).

Pass the token as `Authorization: Bearer tos_...` on every request.

UniversalEvent.description: Frame payload of the SSE event stream. Each frame is serialized as JSON on a `data: ` line.

contextTokens.description: Last API call's input_tokens ≈ current context size.

parent_tool_use_id.description: If produced inside a sub-agent, the tool_use_id of the Agent call that spawned it.

questions.description: Agent-specific question payloads; shape depends on the agent.

get.summary: List workspaces visible to the current caller

post.summary: Create a workspace

get.summary: List messages for a session within a workspace
List sessions for a workspace
Get workspace agent configuration
get.description: Returns the workspace config. `api_key` is always returned as an empty string; the stored value is write-only.

put.description: Empty `api_key` is treated as "do not change". Changing `agent_type` while running rebuilds the container.

get.summary: Get workspace runtime (K8s) status

200.description: Current K8s deployment, service, and pod status

patch.summary: Rename a workspace or change its slug / visibility

delete.summary: Delete a workspace and its underlying instance

post.summary: Start (or rebuild) a workspace instance
post.description: If the configured agent_type differs from the running container image, the deployment is rebuilt before starting.

post.summary: Stop a workspace instance
post.description: Interrupts all active sessions, stops the K8s deployment, and resets session chat status.

post.summary: Snapshot a workspace into a new template
Sync a workspace to its bound template’s latest version

400.description: Workspace is not bound to a template, or is already at latest version

403.description: Latest template version uses skills not visible to the user

get.summary: Get a single session (lightweight, sidebar shape)
get.description: Returns a lite shape with id, name, chat_status, status, and a 40-char preview of the first user message. Use GET /workspaces/:id/sessions for the full ApiSession list.

delete.summary: Delete a session and its messages
delete.description: Interrupts the agent (if running), then drops the session row and its messages. The workspace chat_status cache is refreshed automatically.

post.summary: Star or un-star a session
Interrupt a single session (soft stop, preserves history)

put.summary: Set the queued follow-up message for a session

delete.summary: Drop the queued follow-up message for a session

get.summary: Get aggregate token usage for a workspace
List workspace commands

post.summary: Create a command. Either prompt_id or content must be provided.

delete.summary: Delete a command

post.summary: Enable or disable a template-provided command for this workspace
Start (or continue) a chat turn with a workspace agent
post.description:
Triggers a turn against the workspace agent. Three delivery modes via
`body.mode`:

- `stream` (default) — `text/event-stream` of UniversalEvent frames.
- `sync` — block until the turn ends, return aggregated JSON. Weak for
  long turns; kept for compatibility.
- `async` (recommended) — `202 Accepted` with `{ session_id }` as soon as
  the session exists; the turn keeps running server-side. Poll
  `GET /sessions/:id` and read `GET /messages?session_id=` for results.

When `mode` is absent, the legacy `body.stream` flag (`true` → stream,
`false` → sync) and then the `Accept` header are consulted; default SSE.

SSE events follow the agent UniversalEvent schema. Frame shape is
documented as the `UniversalEvent` component (discriminated on `type`):
session.started, item.started, item.delta, item.completed,
question.requested, session.ended, error. Each frame is emitted on a
single `data: <json>\n\n` line.

200.description:
Chat turn output.

In SSE mode (default) the response is `text/event-stream` carrying
UniversalEvent frames. In JSON mode (`body.stream: false` or
`Accept: application/json`) the server blocks until the turn
ends and returns the aggregated object documented below.

202.description:
Async mode (`body.mode: "async"`) — the turn was accepted and is
running server-side. Returns the session id to poll for results.

name.description: Human-readable folder name; lowercase letters/digits/hyphens, ≤48 chars.

post.summary: Grant another workspace access to this share. Owner only.

delete.summary: Remove a member. Owner can remove any member; a member can remove themselves (leave).

get.summary: Cross-workspace recent active sessions for the current user. Excludes `human` (those go to the drain queue) — covers `agent` and `idle` sorted by last_active_at desc. Used by Home to power the "continue working" rail.
Per-user activity summary for the Home stats sidecar — daily interactions/sessions over the last `days` days (default 30; capped at 365).
Per-user token-usage summary for the Stats app — daily all-in totals, a token-kind composition, and a per-workspace breakdown over the last `days` days (default 30; capped at 365).
List credential metadata for the current user (values are never returned)

put.summary: Upsert a credential. For env injection the name must be a valid env var identifier.

delete.summary: Soft-delete a credential, then hard-delete once all running workspaces reloaded

Run a chat turn in the task coordinator with task-scope MCP authority
post.description:
Same delivery modes as `POST /api/workspaces/:id/chat` (`body.mode`:
`stream` default, `sync`, `async` recommended) — see that route for the
mode semantics. This variant runs the turn in the task coordinator with
task-scope MCP authority.

200.description: Chat turn output (SSE by default, JSON in `sync` mode).

202.description: Async mode — turn accepted and running; returns the session id.

get.summary: Preview an invite link before accepting

post.summary: Accept an invite link and join the team
